description: Basic utility functions including string counting, number range generation, clipboard read/write, time delay (Wait), and human interaction popups (HumanOperation, HumanInput).

function.name: BasicFunction_CountInGivenString
function.description:
Count how many times a specific character or substring appears within a larger string. The search can be case-sensitive or case-insensitive.

When to use: when you need to count occurrences of a character or substring in a given text.
When NOT to use: for general counting of numbers; for trivial cases where the count is obvious.

full_string.description: The non-empty string to search within.

target.description: The non-empty string (character or substring) to count occurrences of.

ignore_case.description: If true, the search is case-insensitive. If false, the search is case-sensitive.

function.name: BasicFunction_CountNumbers
function.description:
Generate a sequence of numbers from a start value to an end value, optionally with a step, and join them with a separator. The sequence includes both start and end if the step direction allows. For a negative step, start must be greater than end to count downwards.

When to use: when you need to generate a range of numbers, especially for large ranges (e.g., from 1 to 10,000).
When NOT to use: for trivial ranges with fewer than 10 numbers (simple mental counting is sufficient).

start.description: The first number in the sequence.

end.description: The last number in the sequence.

step.description: The increment (or decrement if negative). Must not be zero.

separator.description: The string to place between each pair of numbers (e.g., ',', ' - ').

function.name: BasicFunction_WriteToClipboard
function.description:
Write a string to the system clipboard, making it available for pasting in other applications. Replaces any previous clipboard content.

When to use: when you need to copy text to the clipboard for pasting elsewhere.
When NOT to use: when you only need to read the clipboard content (use BasicFunction_ReadFromClipboard instead).

Returns: 'True' on success.

content.description: The non-empty string to write to the clipboard.

function.name: BasicFunction_ReadFromClipboard
function.description:
Retrieve the current textual content of the system clipboard.

When to use: when you need to read what is currently in the clipboard.
When NOT to use: when you need to write to the clipboard (use BasicFunction_WriteToClipboard instead).

Returns: the current clipboard text as a string. Returns an empty string if the clipboard is empty or contains non-text data.

function.name: BasicFunction_Wait
function.description:
Pause execution for a specified number of seconds. The agent blocks and does nothing during this time.

When to use: when you need to wait for a UI animation to finish; when a webpage or application needs time to load; when you need to pace actions to appear human-like; when waiting between retry attempts.
When NOT to use: for sub-second delays (not needed); when you could poll for completion instead.

seconds range: 1 to 6000 (100 minutes max).

function.name: BasicFunction_HumanOperation
function.description:
Show a popup dialog requesting the human user to perform a manual operation. A message explains what to do. The dialog has an OK button — execution blocks until the user clicks it.

When to use: when the agent encounters a CAPTCHA or behavioral verification it cannot solve; when a physical action is required (insert USB key, biometric auth); when a website requires manual interaction the agent cannot simulate.

Returns: confirmation that the user acknowledged the operation.

message.description: The instruction message shown to the user, e.g. 'Please complete the CAPTCHA manually and click OK when done.'

function.name: BasicFunction_HumanInput
function.description:
Show a popup dialog requesting the human user to provide text input. A message explains what information is needed. The dialog has a text field and OK/Cancel buttons.

When to use: when the agent needs information it cannot find automatically (email address, password, account number); when the user needs to provide credentials or secret information; when a decision requires human judgment.

Returns: the text the user entered, or an error if they cancelled.

message.description: The prompt shown to the user, e.g. 'The agent cannot find your saved email address. Please enter the registration email for this website.'

examples.user: Count how many times 'A' appears in 'AaAa' (case-sensitive).
Count how many times 'A' appears in 'AaAa' (case-insensitive).
Generate numbers from 1 to 1000 separated by commas.
Count down from 10 to 2 by steps of -2, separated by ' - '.
Wait 3 seconds for the page to load.
I can't solve this CAPTCHA, please ask the user to do it.

arguments.message: The agent cannot solve the CAPTCHA automatically. Please complete the verification manually, then click OK to continue.

examples.user: I need the user's email to complete this registration.

arguments.message: Please enter the email address to use for registration on this website.
